artifact.description: HALOS Provenance Spec v0.3 — multi-policy governance (governance field changed from object to array)

ai_assistance.description: Compared two design options for multi-policy governance: (A) make governance an array, (B) keep single object and rely on policyEvaluations for additional policies. Recommended option A as cleaner and more honest about the reality.
Drafted v0.3 JSON schema, spec document with migration notes, and updated all 11 examples plus embedded examples to array governance syntax.
Researched versioning conventions across peer specs (SLSA, CycloneDX, OpenAPI, JSON Schema, W3C PROV) to inform decision on version format and draft/alpha suffixes.

decisions.description: Changed governance from a single object to an array of policy references, accepting a breaking change from v0.2
decisions.context: Artifacts routinely fall under multiple concurrent governance policies. The v0.2 single-object form forced authors to pick one policy or concatenate multiple into a string. The realtime-critical-systems example already worked around this by cramming two policies into one string field.

aiInputs.contribution: Proposed two options: (A) governance as array, (B) keep single governance and lean on policyEvaluations. Recommended A because policyEvaluations conflates 'what policies exist' with 'what evaluations were run', and not every governing policy has an automated evaluation.

decisions.rationale: Option A is cleaner and more honest about the reality. The governance field answers 'under what policies was this artifact produced?' — that question is inherently plural. Relying on policyEvaluations to fill the gap conflates policy identity with evaluation results.

decisions.description: Decided to keep x.y version format for halos_version rather than adopting semver or adding draft/alpha suffixes
decisions.context: Question arose whether 0.x breaking changes should bump the major version (semver convention) and whether draft versions should use suffixes like 0.3-draft.

aiInputs.contribution: Researched peer specs: SLSA, CycloneDX, OpenAPI, and JSON Schema all use simple x.y for spec versions. Semver section 4 explicitly defines 0.x as the 'anything may change' pre-stable period. Draft suffixes in version strings would require records to be rewritten when the version goes final.

decisions.rationale: Semver 0.x convention explicitly allows breaking changes at the minor version level. Peer specs in the supply chain ecosystem use the same pattern. Status metadata (Status: Draft/Active) in spec docs is the right place to signal maturity, not the version string inside every record.

decisions.description: Promoted v0.3 to Active status immediately rather than holding as Draft
decisions.context: v0.3 is the only active consumer's current spec. Holding it as Draft while all examples already use it creates an inconsistency.
decisions.rationale: The entire v0.x series is implicitly unstable. Adding a Draft status on top of that is redundant. All examples and landing pages already reference v0.3. Marking it Active reflects reality.

notes: This is the first HALOS provenance record created for a HALOS spec change — the bootstrap moment for self-provenance. Created as part of plan 004 (self-provenance bootstrapping).
